channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Northern California's rare tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Northern California was under a rare and brief tsunami warning alert Thursday that tested local emergency notification systems after a 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook part of the state.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ruled that the U.S. Naval Academy can continue considering race in its admissions process, finding that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of people fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country’s third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday, positioning themselves for an assault on a potentially major prize in their march against President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Recordings: Woman says slain Kentucky judge was involved in alleged sextortion scandal
item.description: In the recording, a woman claims to have seen video of Mullins engaging in sexual acts in his office.
item.dc:creator: Allison Hope Weiner, WGN 9

item.title: Indianapolis police officers are acquitted in the 2022 death of a man at his parents’ home
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers were acquitted of manslaughter and other charges Friday in the death of a man after officers shocked him with a Taser and restrained him face down while handcuffing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: JetBlue nixing routes between multiple major cities, reducing several others
item.description: Passengers relying on JetBlue routes between certain American cities may need to make alternate travel plans in the near future.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Each state's 'most popular' Christmas song, according to Google Trends — Utah's might surprise you
item.description: Which Christmas songs do we just love to love, at least until we get sick of Christmas music and forget about it until late into the next calendar year? Google just might have the answer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Millions will lose health coverage if ACA subsidies expire: CBO
item.description: If Affordable Care Act subsidies are allowed to expire at the end of 2025 millions of people will lose their insurance and premiums will jump, according to a Congressional Budget Office report.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, KRON4

item.title: USDA requires bulk testing of raw milk supply for bird flu virus
item.description: The nation’s milk supply must be tested for the bird flu virus H5N1 under a new order announced Friday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – here's why.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USDA orders nationwide testing of milk for bird flu to halt the virus
item.description: The U.S. government has ordered testing of the nation’s milk supply for bird flu to better monitor the spread of the virus in dairy cows.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Remains of Pennsylvania woman who disappeared near sinkhole may have been found, coroners say
item.description: A deputy coroner in Westmoreland County, Pennsylvania, said searchers believe they have found the remains of Elizabeth Pollard. Pollard went missing while looking for her cat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Data reveals the 'most popular' Christmas song in Nevada
item.description: Which Christmas songs do we just love to love, at least until we get sick of Christmas music and forget about it until late into the next calendar year? Google just might have the answer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen killed, naked man arrested at California High School
item.description: A 24-year-old man, possibly naked and wielding an ax, was arrested in connection with the killing of a teenager in Newbury Park on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Each state's 'most popular' Christmas song, according to Google Trends data
item.description: Which Christmas songs do we just love to love, at least until we get sick of Christmas music and forget about it until late into the next calendar year? Google just might have the answer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom visits border, slams Trump on planned deportations, tariffs
item.description: "No state will be more impacted by that, no region will be impacted by that, small businesses that will lose customers, jobs that will be lost, inflationary impacts on every single one of you," said Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: At a sinkhole above an abandoned Pennsylvania coal mine, hopes of a rescue dwindle
item.description: Three days after a Pennsylvania woman searching for her cat disappeared at the chasm of a three-story deep sinkhole, the digging crews were back at it Thursday, hoping for a miracle but expecting to recover her remains.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tsunami warning canceled after strong California earthquake
item.description: The National Weather Service canceled a tsunami warning Thursday for a large stretch of California coastline, saying the threat had passed following a strong earthquake near Eureka.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: College students unearth mastodon fossil near St. Louis
item.description: Long before there was a Great River Road, about 27,000-and-a-half years ago, a mastodon once stood atop the bluffs overlooking the Mississippi River. Faculty and students at Principia College have discovered the specimen.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Clark, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois homeowner feels underground mine is splitting her house in two
item.description: About 330,000 Illinois homes sit on top of old abandoned mines. Some say their homes are sinking because of it.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, KRON4

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WGN 9

item.title: 2 kindergarteners wounded and gunman dead after shooting at California religious school
item.description: PALERMO, Calif. (AP) — Two children were in “extremely critical condition” Wednesday after being shot at a tiny religious K-8 school in Northern California and the gunman died at the scene, apparently from a self-inflicted gunshot, police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California farmers are hopeful Trump administration will deliver more water to fields
item.description: Since winning the election, President-elect Donald Trump has been talking about immigration, border security and government efficiency. But in California farm country, his comments about water are also getting top attention.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Billionaire who performed the first private spacewalk is Trump's pick to lead NASA
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has tapped a tech billionaire who performed the first private spacewalk to lead NASA. Trump announced on Wednesday his choice for NASA administrator.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO fatally shot in 'targeted attack' in NYC, officials say
item.description: The CEO of UnitedHealthcare was killed in a Midtown shooting Wednesday morning, sources have confirmed to Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KTLA 5

item.title: Frontier Airlines to roll out first-class seating, other perks in 2025
item.description: Frontier Airlines will offer first-class seating for the first time in its history starting in late 2025, the company announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, Fox 5

item.title: Former SmileDirectClub customers may be eligible for a refund
item.description: When SmileDirectClub shut down a year ago, scores of existing customers of the teeth-straightening company were left in limbo. Now, tens of thousands are set to get some relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jury deliberations begin in veteran Daniel Penny's trial over using chokehold on Jordan Neely
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors began deliberating and soon revisited some of their legal instructions Tuesday in the trial of a military veteran charged with using a fatal chokehold to subdue a New York subway rider whose behavior was alarming other passengers.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida woman who left boyfriend in zipped suitcase for hours sentenced in his death
item.description: She was “shocked” by the guilty verdict and thought maybe the jury would convict her of a lesser charge, her lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5
